title: OSF Constraint Block (next/, Option C — JSON-as-SSOT)
description: Meta-schema for the 'constraints' attribute facet added by the SDC-inspired augmentation track. Validates the STRUCTURE of constraint rules embedded in profiles/machines/*.json (or other profile categories). The actual TYPE-correctness of predicate values against the referenced attribute.dataType is enforced by next/ci/lint-constraints.mjs (separate lint step — JSON Schema cannot express the cross-reference). One meta-schema for all asset types; constraint instances live per profile and inherit via parentType.

description.description: Human-readable rule description (becomes sh:message in the SHACL build artifact)

when.description: Optional precondition — if absent, 'require' is checked unconditionally

require.description: Must hold when 'when' is true (or always, if 'when' is absent)

severity.description: error -> sh:Violation + opens steward queue; warning -> sh:Warning + telemetry-only

discrepancyClass.description: Pins the emitted discrepancy class. Requires 'constraint' in profiles/intelligence/discrepancy.json#discrepancy_class enum AND in services/discrepancy-engine/src/types.ts.

since.description: Date the constraint was introduced — used to gate alerts on legacy data

concern.description: ISA-95-grounded nature of this constraint. 'operations' = a deviation in MOM execution (real-world: schedule slip, quality disposition, equipment rule) -> routed to the OT/Operations lane + own subject/store. 'data' = an inconsistency/staleness of an ISA-95 information object across systems/levels -> the data-steward cockpit (ZENTRAL). Optional during rollout (classified autonomously, see services/concern-classifier); engine treats an absent value as unclassified (stays in the data lane). Will become required once every constraint is classified.

mom_category.description: For concern='operations' only — the ISA-95 Part 3 MOM operations category that owns the deviation. Defaults from the profile's isa95.momDomain when that is one of the four MOM categories; declared here when it differs (e.g. order_overdue lives in a Level-4 'business' SalesOrder profile but is a 'production' scheduling concern).

accountable_role.description: Organisational role/function ACCOUNTABLE for this kind of deviation (RACI: accountable). Customer-maintained in the SSOT — we do not define it, we route on it. The episode lands in this value's role queue; the concrete person is resolved (later) from an external org/shift-roster system. Examples: 'Production Control', 'Quality Assurance', 'Production Planning', 'Maintenance', 'Process Engineering'. Optional during rollout; absent -> unassigned role queue.

deviation_class.description: Orthogonal sub-classification of an operations deviation by its functional KIND, independent of mom_category — lets a specific persona filter their slice. 'recipe' = a machine recipe / setpoint (Einstellparameter) band deviation (ISA-88 recipe management), surfaced as its own class on the Operations page and routed to the recipe-owning role (Process Engineering). Optional; absent -> a generic operations finding. Extend the enum to add further classes.

attr.description: References an attribute.name within the same profile (or an inherited one). next/ci/lint-constraints.mjs resolves this against the profile's attribute array.

op.description: Comparison operator. 'in' expects an array value. 'between' expects [lo, hi].

value.description: Literal comparison value. Type is checked at lint-time against the referenced attribute.dataType (Int32/Float -> number; String -> string; Boolean -> boolean). For op='in' must be array; for op='between' must be [lo, hi]. Mutually exclusive with valueAttr/valueFrom.

valueAttr.description: Compare attr against ANOTHER attribute's live value instead of a literal (attr-vs-attr). The reserved token '$now' resolves to current time (temporal rules like overdue). Evaluated centrally by the it-evaluator; the edge OT detector uses literals only.

valueFrom.description: Reference the RHS to an EXTERNAL spec value supplied at runtime — the active recipe / ISA-95 ProductDefinition for the entity. The recipe (Definition leg: Definition->Request->Response) carries product-specific setpoints + tolerance bands, so the SAME rule yields different limits per article (e.g. 'recipe:temp_z1_band' -> a [lo,hi] band for op='between', or a setpoint for op='lte'). Resolved CENTRALLY by the it-evaluator from the active-recipe binding (running FO -> Article x Machine x SetupMode); the edge OT detector ignores it (literals only). DORMANT until a recipe source binds the value (outcome 'unknown' -> no false raise). This RESERVES the hook only — recipe SOURCING/management is built when a MES/spec source exists, wherever the recipe comes from. Mutually exclusive with value/valueAttr.
